Document: # Vehicle and Motorised Assets If a vehicle or other general motorised asset is deployed for any reason, the following regulations apply. - The Commander or Adjutant may override any decisions relating to vehicle or other motorised assets. - Authority over ground vehicles (including tanks, Humvees, APCs, and similar assets) rests with the Vehicle Commander. If no Vehicle Commander is assigned, responsibility defaults to the Engineering Officer. - Authority over air vehicles rests with pilots, followed by the rest of the flight crew if no pilot is assigned. - Anyone may operate APCs, tanks, IFVs, and similar vehicles, provided they are capable of using the vehicle and have permission from either the Vehicle Commander, Engineering Officer, Adjutant, or Commander, and so long as operating the vehicle does not breach any deployment SOP. - Medical and Logistics staff may utilise a vehicle for medical frontline duties or resupplying troops on the frontline, provided it does not violate their deployment SOPs. Only one member of either department may operate or occupy the vehicle at any given time for this purpose (EX. a single doctor in a medical APC treating wounded on the frontline, or a single logistics staff member in a Humvee delivering supplies to the frontline). - Synthetics may be utilised as drivers but never as gunners for a vehicle. - Command staff may utilise vehicles so long as it does not violate their deployment SOP’s. - Command staff may utilise the Command type ARC for deployment to the FOB and, with the commander's permission utilize it in deployment to the frontline.
